results.description: Signal handlers (SIGSEGV, SIGABRT, SIGBUS, SIGFPE, SIGTERM, SIGINT) are registered
results.evidence: Lines 664-665: signal.signal for SIGSEGV, SIGABRT, SIGBUS, SIGFPE via loop. Lines 672-673: signal.signal for SIGTERM, SIGINT via loop. Both handlers call _save_crash_recovery before exit.

results.description: Crash recovery checkpoint is saved at epoch start and every N batches
results.evidence: Line 677: _save_crash_recovery(ckpt_dir, 'epoch_start') before first batch. Lines 946-950: periodic checkpoint at step % _checkpoint_interval == 0 (_checkpoint_interval=50). Also saves named checkpoint epoch_N_batch_M.pth.

results.description: NaN/Inf batches are skipped correctly without crashing
results.evidence: Three-stage guard: (1) lines 705-717: image NaN check before forward pass; (2) lines 869-886: loss isfinite check after criterion call, skips batch and zeroes grads; (3) lines 772-777: sequence batch loss isfinite check. nan_skips counter tracks all skips.

results.description: Data integrity check (finite images) is performed before forward pass
results.evidence: Lines 705-717: torch.isfinite check on max-abs of images tensor, skips batch if non-finite detected, skips step 0 (first batch can have weird init values).

results.description: GPU memory snapshot is taken every 10 batches
results.evidence: Lines 684-691: torch.cuda.memory_allocated and torch.cuda.memory_reserved logged every 10 batches (step > 0 && step % 10 == 0).

results.description: CPU RAM watchdog checks available memory every 50 batches
results.evidence: Lines 952-977: reads /proc/meminfo, extracts MemAvailable, Buffers, Cached, logs warning if avail_gb < 2.0, runs every 50 batches ((step + 1) % 50 == 0).

results.description: DataLoader worker health is checked every 100 batches
results.evidence: Original code used loader._workers_check_alive() which is NOT a real PyTorch DataLoader method (doesn't exist). Fixed: replaced with non-blocking queue probe on loader._worker_result_queue as a health indicator. Real crash detection is via exception handling in main() which catches BrokenPipeError/FileNotFoundError and rebuilds the loader (lines 1924-1945). Worker check now runs every 100 batches as intended.

results.description: Validation loop does not crash on edge cases (empty predictions, all same class)
results.evidence: compute_activity_metrics (evaluate.py lines 631-649): returns zeros for all metrics when arrays are empty. PSR transition: lines 1586-1591 handle n_gt==0 && n_pred==0 case (uninformative) by appending NaN. Clip-level NaN guard: line 587-591 handles pred_clip_valid empty or all-NaN.

issues_found.description: loader._workers_check_alive() does not exist in PyTorch DataLoader
issues_found.fix_applied: Replaced non-existent _workers_check_alive() with a non-blocking get_nowait() on loader._worker_result_queue as a health probe. Real worker crash detection remains via the exception handling retry loop in main() which correctly rebuilds the loader on BrokenPipeError/FileNotFoundError.
issues_found.after: Non-blocking queue probe on _worker_result_queue + comment explaining real crash detection is via exception path
